Hierarchy view improvements in GitHub Projects You now have several improvements to hierarchy view in GitHub Projects based on your feedback: Filter sub-issues: You can now filter sub-issues using syntax…

The post [Hierarchy view improvements and file uploads in issue forms](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-03-05-hierarchy-view-improvements-and-file-uploads-in-issue-forms) appeared first on [The GitHub Blog](https://github.blog).
EnhancedContent:
## Hierarchy view improvements in GitHub Projects

You now have several improvements to [hierarchy view in GitHub Projects](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-01-15-hierarchy-view-now-available-in-github-projects/) based on your feedback:

- **Filter sub-issues:** You can now filter sub-issues using syntax like `sub-issues.is:open`
to narrow your view to sub-issues matching specific criteria.
- **Avoid showing duplicate issues:** Items that appear in multiple places in the hierarchy are now deduplicated in most cases, keeping your views cleaner and easier to scan.
- **Preserve expand/collapse state:** When you expand or collapse sub-issues, the state is now saved so the hierarchy stays the way you left it when you return to the view.

For feedback or questions on hierarchy view, join the discussion within [GitHub Community](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/184225).

## Issue forms now support a new upload field type

You can now add an **upload** field in issue forms to require key files like logs, screenshots, and crash reports up front, so issues come in with the context you need.

## Move to next iteration in bulk

In GitHub Projects, you can now **bulk-move all items from one iteration to another** directly from the group header menu. When your view is grouped by an iteration field, select “Move items to…” to quickly roll over unfinished work or reschedule items to a different sprint.
Title: Hierarchy view improvements and file uploads in issue forms
Author: Allison
